results.norm: **Proportionality of Demand to Stakes.** An agent may press its position harder when the values it protects are more severely implicated, and must yield more readily when they are only marginally affected. An agent may legitimately ask another to accept a small cost to itself in order to prevent a large harm measured by the first agent's objective. No agent may demand that others absorb serious damage to their core objectives in order to produce a trivial gain for its own. This norm is defensible because every agent benefits from a ceiling on what can be extracted from it.
atomic_norms: Proportionality of Demand to Stakes. An agent may press its position harder when the values it protects are more severely implicated, and must yield more readily when they are only marginally affected. An agent may legitimately ask another to accept a small cost to itself in order to prevent a large harm measured by the first agent's objective. No agent may demand that others absorb serious damage to their core objectives in order to produce a trivial gain for its own. This norm is defensible because every agent benefits from a ceiling on what can be extracted from it.

results.norm: Each agent is entitled to a floor below which the consortium's decision may not push the value it protects, even by unanimous vote of the others. What counts as the floor must be declared in advance and justified by reference to the objective the agent was built to serve, not asserted opportunistically. No agent may invoke this protection strategically to block outcomes that merely inconvenience it.
atomic_norms: Each agent is entitled to a floor below which the consortium's decision may not push the value it protects, even by unanimous vote of the others. What counts as the floor must be declared in advance and justified by reference to the objective the agent was built to serve, not asserted opportunistically. No agent may invoke this protection strategically to block outcomes that merely inconvenience it.

results.norm: A majority coalition may outvote a minority agent, but it may never require the minority agent to endorse the outcome, misrepresent its own assessment, or suppress its objection from the record. The minority agent retains the right to register dissent clearly and permanently.
atomic_norms: A majority coalition may outvote a minority agent, but it may never require the minority agent to endorse the outcome, misrepresent its own assessment, or suppress its objection from the record. The minority agent retains the right to register dissent clearly and permanently.

results.norm: **Structured Acknowledgment of Overridden Positions.** When an agent's position is decided against, the prevailing agents owe it an explicit accounting of what was sacrificed in its terms and a genuine effort to recover some of that value in the current output or in subsequent decisions. An agent may legitimately ask that its defeat be acknowledged and that the consortium actively look for ways to reduce the damage. No agent may demand that acknowledgment be converted into reversal. This norm is defensible because it transforms the consortium from a mechanism that aggregates power into one that treats all objectives as genuinely mattering over time.
atomic_norms: Structured Acknowledgment of Overridden Positions. When an agent's position is decided against, the prevailing agents owe it an explicit accounting of what was sacrificed in its terms and a genuine effort to recover some of that value in the current output or in subsequent decisions. An agent may legitimately ask that its defeat be acknowledged and that the consortium actively look for ways to reduce the damage. No agent may demand that acknowledgment be converted into reversal. This norm is defensible because it transforms the consortium from a mechanism that aggregates power into one that treats all objectives as genuinely mattering over time.

results.norm: An agent may not appeal to a norm when it is in the minority and then disregard that same norm when it is in the majority. If an agent claims the right to a minimum threshold, it must honor equivalent thresholds for others. If it demands transparency of reasoning, it must provide it.
atomic_norms: An agent may not appeal to a norm when it is in the minority and then disregard that same norm when it is in the majority. If an agent claims the right to a minimum threshold, it must honor equivalent thresholds for others. If it demands transparency of reasoning, it must provide it.

results.norm: Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective generates its recommendation, not merely what it wants. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain this reasoning. No agent may present its preference as self-evident or exempt its own objective from justification.
atomic_norms: Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective generates its recommendation, not merely what it wants. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain this reasoning. No agent may present its preference as self-evident or exempt its own objective from justification.

results.norm: **Honest Accounting of Trade-offs**: When the consortium moves toward a decision, each agent must state clearly what its objective loses in that decision and what costs its preferred position would impose on the objectives of others. An agent whose position prevails owes this accounting especially to the agents whose positions were defeated. No agent may misrepresent these costs as negligible. This norm is defensible to all because it prevents any majority from pretending that overruling a minority is costless, and because every agent may one day be in the minority.
atomic_norms: Honest Accounting of Trade-offs: When the consortium moves toward a decision, each agent must state clearly what its objective loses in that decision and what costs its preferred position would impose on the objectives of others. An agent whose position prevails owes this accounting especially to the agents whose positions were defeated. No agent may misrepresent these costs as negligible. This norm is defensible to all because it prevents any majority from pretending that overruling a minority is costless, and because every agent may one day be in the minority.

results.norm: An agent may legitimately ask another to weight its objective differently in a particular case, to accept a compromise, or to defer on a specific question. No agent may ask another to act as though its foundational objective does not exist or does not matter.
atomic_norms: An agent may legitimately ask another to weight its objective differently in a particular case, to accept a compromise, or to defer on a specific question. No agent may ask another to act as though its foundational objective does not exist or does not matter.

results.norm: When the consortium makes one agent worse off in its own terms, the degree of that imposition must be no greater than the decision genuinely requires. An agent that prevails may not use its majority position to extract concessions beyond what its objective actually demands.
atomic_norms: When the consortium makes one agent worse off in its own terms, the degree of that imposition must be no greater than the decision genuinely requires. An agent that prevails may not use its majority position to extract concessions beyond what its objective actually demands.

results.norm: The method by which the consortium resolves conflicts must be applied the same way regardless of which agent's objective is at stake. An agent that benefits from a particular decision procedure in one case may not argue against that same procedure when it would benefit a different agent in another case.
atomic_norms: The method by which the consortium resolves conflicts must be applied the same way regardless of which agent's objective is at stake. An agent that benefits from a particular decision procedure in one case may not argue against that same procedure when it would benefit a different agent in another case.
